name: Pork Chops with Capers
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle the pork chops with pepper. Heat vegetable oil in a nonstick frying pan. When the oil is very hot, place the chops in the pan and brown them well on each side for about 5 minutes per side. Lower the heat and continue cooking until thoroughly cooked through, another 20 minutes. Turn the chops frequently as they cook to ensure even browning. Remove the chops to a warm platter and cover them to keep warm. Discard all the fat in the pan.
2. Add the capers to the pan and sauté briefly. Add the onions and continue sautéing. Stir the capers and onions together, then brown slightly. Add the garlic and sauté briefly. Add the vinegar and use a spatula to stir until any meat particles dissolve in the pan. Add the stock and tomato, using any accumulated liquid around the chops.
3. Allow the sauce to boil vigorously until most of the moisture has evaporated. Stir in the Dijon mustard and pour the mixture over the sauce immediately. Garnish with chopped parsley.
